crypto.name: Bitcoin
crypto.description: Founded by Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008, Bitcoin is the first decentralized cryptocurrency.
crypto.history: Bitcoin's genesis block was mined in 2009. It has since become the dominant digital store of value.

crypto.name: Ethereum
crypto.description: Launched in 2015 by Vitalik Buterin, Ethereum is a decentralized, open-source blockchain with smart contract functionality.
crypto.history: Ethereum transitioned to Proof-of-Stake in 2022 (The Merge) to become 99% more energy efficient.

crypto.name: Solana
crypto.description: Solana is a high-performance blockchain supporting builders around the world.
crypto.history: Launched in 2020 by Anatoly Yakovenko, Solana focuses on high throughput and low latency.

crypto.name: BNB
crypto.description: BNB is the ecosystem token for Binance, the world's largest crypto exchange.
crypto.history: Originally launched on Ethereum in 2017, it now powers the BNB Smart Chain (BSC).

crypto.name: Ripple
crypto.description: XRP is the native token for the Ripple payment protocol.
crypto.history: Founded in 2012, Ripple aims to enable instant, low-cost international payments.

crypto.name: Cardano
crypto.description: Cardano is a proof-of-stake blockchain platform focused on peer-reviewed research.
crypto.history: Founded by Charles Hoskinson in 2015, it uses the Ouroboros consensus protocol.

crypto.name: Dogecoin
crypto.description: Originally created as a joke, Dogecoin has become one of the most popular cryptocurrencies.
crypto.history: Created in 2013 by Billy Markus and Jackson Palmer, Dogecoin gained massive cultural relevance.

crypto.name: Avalanche
crypto.description: Avalanche is a smart contracts platform focused on speed and low costs.
crypto.history: Launched in 2020 by Ava Labs, founded by Cornell professor Emin Gün Sirer.

crypto.name: Polkadot
crypto.description: Polkadot enables cross-blockchain transfers of any type of data or asset.
crypto.history: Founded by Gavin Wood, co-founder of Ethereum, in 2016.

crypto.name: Polygon
crypto.description: Polygon is a Layer 2 scaling solution for Ethereum.
crypto.history: Founded in 2017 in India, it provides faster and cheaper transactions on Ethereum.

crypto.name: Chainlink
crypto.description: Chainlink provides tamper-proof data feeds for smart contracts.
crypto.history: Founded by Sergey Nazarov in 2017, it pioneered decentralized oracle networks.

crypto.name: Shiba Inu
crypto.description: Shiba Inu is a Dogecoin-inspired meme token with a growing ecosystem.
crypto.history: Created anonymously in August 2020 as an experiment in community building.

stocks.name: Apple Inc.
stocks.description: Apple is a global leader in consumer electronics, software, and services.
stocks.history: Founded by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne in 1976. The iPhone changed the world in 2007.

stocks.name: Microsoft Corp.
stocks.description: A global leader in software, cloud computing (Azure), and personal computers.
stocks.history: Founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen in 1975. Dominated the PC market with Windows.

stocks.name: Tesla, Inc.
stocks.description: Tesla specializes in electric vehicles, energy storage, and solar panel manufacturing.
stocks.history: Founded in 2003 by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning; Elon Musk became chairman in 2004.

stocks.name: NVIDIA Corp.
stocks.description: A leader in graphics processing units (GPUs) and AI computing.
stocks.history: Founded in 1993, NVIDIA revolutionized gaming and now powers the AI revolution.

stocks.name: Alphabet (Google)
stocks.description: Alphabet is the parent company of Google, the world's largest search engine.
stocks.history: Founded by Larry Page and Sergey Brin at Stanford in 1998.

stocks.name: Amazon.com
stocks.description: The world's largest e-commerce and cloud computing company.
stocks.history: Founded by Jeff Bezos in 1994 as an online bookstore, now a tech giant.

stocks.name: Meta Platforms
stocks.description: Meta owns Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and is building the metaverse.
stocks.history: Founded by Mark Zuckerberg in 2004 at Harvard. Rebranded to Meta in 2021.

stocks.name: Netflix Inc.
stocks.description: The world's leading streaming entertainment service.
stocks.history: Founded in 1997 by Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph. Pioneered streaming video.

stocks.name: Advanced Micro Devices
stocks.description: AMD designs CPUs and GPUs competing with Intel and NVIDIA.
stocks.history: Founded in 1969, AMD's Ryzen and EPYC processors transformed the chip market.

stocks.name: Intel Corp.
stocks.description: The world's largest semiconductor chip maker by revenue.
stocks.history: Founded in 1968 by Gordon Moore and Robert Noyce. Pioneer of x86 architecture.

stocks.name: Reliance Industries
stocks.description: India's largest conglomerate spanning energy, retail, and telecom (Jio).
stocks.history: Founded by Dhirubhai Ambani in 1966. Now led by Mukesh Ambani, India's richest person.

stocks.name: Tata Consultancy Services
stocks.description: India's largest IT services company and a Tata Group subsidiary.
stocks.history: Founded in 1968, TCS is a global leader in IT consulting and business solutions.

stocks.name: Infosys Ltd.
stocks.description: A global leader in digital services and consulting.
stocks.history: Founded in 1981 by Narayana Murthy and six engineers with $250.

stocks.name: HDFC Bank
stocks.description: India's largest private sector bank by market capitalisation.
stocks.history: Incorporated in 1994, HDFC Bank merged with HDFC Ltd in 2023.

stocks.name: ICICI Bank
stocks.description: One of India's largest private sector banks.
stocks.history: Originally promoted in 1994 by ICICI Limited, an Indian financial institution.

stocks.name: State Bank of India
stocks.description: India's largest public sector bank with the most extensive branch network.
stocks.history: Traces its roots to the Bank of Calcutta, founded in 1806. Nationalized in 1955.

stocks.name: Bharti Airtel
stocks.description: India's second largest telecom operator with a presence in 18 countries.
stocks.history: Founded by Sunil Bharti Mittal in 1995, now operates across Asia and Africa.

stocks.name: ITC Limited
stocks.description: A diversified conglomerate in FMCG, hotels, packaging, and agribusiness.
stocks.history: Originally Imperial Tobacco Company (1910), now a multi-business conglomerate.

stocks.name: Hindustan Unilever
stocks.description: India's largest fast-moving consumer goods company.
stocks.history: A subsidiary of Unilever, operating in India since 1933.

stocks.name: Wipro Ltd.
stocks.description: A leading global IT consulting and outsourcing company.
stocks.history: Founded by M.H. Hasham Premji in 1945, pivoted to IT under Azim Premji.

stocks.name: Tata Motors
stocks.description: India's largest automobile manufacturer, owner of Jaguar Land Rover.
stocks.history: Part of the Tata Group, founded in 1945. Acquired JLR from Ford in 2008.

stocks.name: Adani Enterprises
stocks.description: The flagship company of the Adani Group, spanning energy, ports, and infrastructure.
stocks.history: Founded by Gautam Adani in 1988, now one of India's largest conglomerates.

stocks.name: Larsen & Toubro
stocks.description: India's largest engineering and construction conglomerate.
stocks.history: Founded in 1938 by Danish engineers Henning Holck-Larsen and Søren Kristian Toubro.

stocks.name: Bajaj Finance
stocks.description: India's largest non-banking financial company (NBFC).
stocks.history: Part of the Bajaj Group, it became a major consumer lending platform.

stocks.name: Maruti Suzuki
stocks.description: India's largest passenger car manufacturer.
stocks.history: A joint venture between Govt. of India and Suzuki Motor Corp., started in 1981.

stocks.name: Axis Bank
stocks.description: India's third largest private sector bank.
stocks.history: Formerly UTI Bank, renamed Axis Bank in 2007.

stocks.name: Titan Company
stocks.description: India's leading consumer lifestyle company known for Tanishq jewelry and Titan watches.
stocks.history: A Tata Group company, founded in 1984 as a joint venture with TIDCO.

stocks.name: Sun Pharma
stocks.description: India's largest pharmaceutical company and the world's fourth largest specialty generics company.
stocks.history: Founded by Dilip Shanghvi in 1983 with just 5 products.

stocks.name: Asian Paints
stocks.description: India's largest paint company and Asia's third largest paint company.
stocks.history: Founded in 1942 by four friends in a garage in Mumbai.

stocks.name: Nestle India
stocks.description: Indian subsidiary of Nestlé, makers of Maggi noodles and Nescafé.
stocks.history: Nestlé began operations in India in 1961. Maggi is India's most iconic instant noodle brand.

stocks.name: Tata Steel
stocks.description: One of India's largest steel producers and a Tata Group company.
stocks.history: Founded by Jamsetji Tata in 1907, India's first private sector steel plant.

stocks.name: HCL Technologies
stocks.description: A leading global IT services company.
stocks.history: Founded by Shiv Nadar in 1976, HCL is one of India's top technology firms.

stocks.name: Power Grid Corp.
stocks.description: India's largest electric power transmission utility.
stocks.history: Founded in 1989 as a central government enterprise.

stocks.name: Coal India
stocks.description: The world's largest coal-producing company.
stocks.history: Formed in 1975 as a government enterprise, listed on BSE and NSE.

stocks.name: Oil & Natural Gas Corp.
stocks.description: India's largest crude oil and natural gas company.
stocks.history: Established in 1956, ONGC is India's flagship energy exploration PSU.

forex.name: Euro / US Dollar
forex.description: The most traded currency pair in the world.
forex.history: Represents the relative strength of the Eurozone vs the United States economies.

forex.name: British Pound / US Dollar
forex.description: Known as 'Cable', referencing the transatlantic cables used for quotes.
forex.history: One of the oldest currency pairs traded in the global markets.

forex.name: US Dollar / Japanese Yen
forex.description: The second most traded currency pair globally.
forex.history: A key indicator of Asian economic health and US monetary policy.

forex.name: US Dollar / Swiss Franc
forex.description: Known as 'The Swissie', it represents the safe-haven Swiss economy.
forex.history: Switzerland's neutrality and banking secrecy make CHF a safe-haven currency.

forex.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
forex.description: Known as 'The Aussie', closely tied to commodity prices.
forex.history: Australia's economy is heavily resource-based, making AUD a commodity currency.

forex.name: US Dollar / Canadian Dollar
forex.description: Known as 'The Loonie', heavily influenced by oil prices.
forex.history: Canada is a major oil exporter, closely tying CAD to crude oil movements.

forex.name: US Dollar / Indian Rupee
forex.description: Tracks the Indian Rupee against the US Dollar.
forex.history: A key pair for Indian traders; RBI manages INR via forex interventions.

forex.name: British Pound / Indian Rupee
forex.description: Historical currency pair reflecting UK-India trade relations.
forex.history: One of the important cross-currency pairs for Indian forex traders.

commodities.name: Gold
commodities.description: The ultimate global store of value and safe-haven asset.
commodities.history: Used as currency and jewelry for millennia; remains a hedge against inflation.

commodities.name: Silver
commodities.description: A precious metal used in industry and as a store of value.
commodities.history: Known as 'the poor man's gold', silver has industrial and investment demand.

commodities.name: Copper
commodities.description: The 'Doctor Copper' of commodities, reflecting global economic health.
commodities.history: Crucial for electrical wiring and modern infrastructure.

commodities.name: Brent Crude Oil
commodities.description: The international benchmark for crude oil pricing.
commodities.history: Named after the Brent oil field in the North Sea, it sets prices globally.

commodities.name: WTI Crude Oil
commodities.description: The US benchmark for crude oil, traded on NYMEX.
commodities.history: West Texas Intermediate is the most actively traded oil futures contract.

commodities.name: Natural Gas
commodities.description: A key energy commodity used for heating and electricity.
commodities.history: Natural gas prices are driven by weather, storage levels, and production.
